title: Judge Approves $1.5 Billion Anthropic Settlement Over Pirated Books Used To Train Claude - Slashdot
excerpt: A federal judge has approved Anthropic's $1.5 billion copyright settlement over pirated books used to train its Claude chatbot, with authors and publishers set to receive about $3,000 per book. The case produced a mixed ruling for the AI industry: training on copyrighted books was found not to be il...
content_html: A federal judge has approved Anthropic's $1.5 billion copyright settlement over pirated books used to train its Claude chatbot, with authors and publishers set to receive about $3,000 per book. The case produced a mixed ruling for the AI industry: training on copyrighted books was found not to be illegal, but Anthropic's use of pirated copies from shadow libraries was. The Associated Press reports: District Judge Araceli Martinez-Olguin said in a Monday ruling that the class-action settlement provides "meaningful relief" to affected authors and publishers. About 91% of the more than 482,000 books covered by the ruling have been claimed by authors or publishers who are now due payment. Plaintiff attorney Justin Nelson said in a statement that the settlement was "the largest known copyright recovery in history. We look forward to making distributions to the Class as promptly as possible."

The Associated Press reports: District Judge Araceli Martinez-Olguin said in a Monday ruling that the class-action settlement provides "meaningful relief" to affected authors and publishers. Plaintiff attorney Justin Nelson said in a statement that the settlement was "the largest known copyright recovery in history. We look forward to making distributions to the Class as promptly as possible."

The case produced a mixed ruling for the AI industry: training on copyrighted books was found not to be illegal, but Anthropic's use of pirated copies from shadow libraries was. The Associated Press reports: District Judge Araceli Martinez-Olguin said in a Monday ruling that the class-action settlement provides "meaningful relief" to affected authors and publishers. About 91% of the more than 482,000 books covered by the ruling have been claimed by authors or publishers who are now due payment. Plaintiff attorney Justin Nelson said in a statement that the settlement was "the largest known copyright recovery in history. We look forward to making distributions to the Class as promptly as possible."

sentences.en: A federal judge has approved Anthropic's $1.5 billion copyright settlement over pirated books used to train its Claude chatbot, with authors and publishers set to receive about $3,000 per book.
sentences.zh: 一名联邦法官批准了 Anthropic 就用于训练其 Claude 聊天机器人的盗版书籍达成的 15 亿美元版权和解协议，作者和出版商将获得每本书约 3,000 美元的赔偿。

sentences.en: The case produced a mixed ruling for the AI industry: training on copyrighted books was found not to be illegal, but Anthropic's use of pirated copies from shadow libraries was.
sentences.zh: 该案对人工智能行业产生了好坏参半的裁决：对受版权保护的书籍进行培训并不违法，但 Anthropic 使用影子图书馆的盗版副本则违法。

sentences.en: The Associated Press reports: District Judge Araceli Martinez-Olguin said in a Monday ruling that the class-action settlement provides "meaningful relief" to affected authors and publishers.
sentences.zh: 美联社报道：地区法官阿拉塞利·马丁内斯-奥尔金在周一的裁决中表示，集体诉讼和解协议为受影响的作者和出版商提供了“有意义的救济”。

sentences.en: About 91% of the more than 482,000 books covered by the ruling have been claimed by authors or publishers who are now due payment.
sentences.zh: 该裁决涵盖的 482,000 多本书中，约 91% 已被到期付款的作者或出版商认领。

sentences.en: Plaintiff attorney Justin Nelson said in a statement that the settlement was "the largest known copyright recovery in history.
sentences.zh: 原告律师贾斯汀·尼尔森在一份声明中表示，此次和解是“历史上已知的最大的版权追回案”。

sentences.en: We look forward to making distributions to the Class as promptly as possible."
sentences.zh: 我们期待尽快向班级分发。”

content_html_zh: 一名联邦法官批准了 Anthropic 就用于训练其 Claude 聊天机器人的盗版书籍达成的 15 亿美元版权和解协议，作者和出版商将获得每本书约 3,000 美元的赔偿。 该案对人工智能行业产生了好坏参半的裁决：对受版权保护的书籍进行培训并不违法，但 Anthropic 使用影子图书馆的盗版副本则违法。 美联社报道：地区法官阿拉塞利·马丁内斯-奥尔金在周一的裁决中表示，集体诉讼和解协议为受影响的作者和出版商提供了“有意义的救济”。 该裁决涵盖的 482,000 多本书中，约 91% 已被到期付款的作者或出版商认领。 原告律师贾斯汀·尼尔森在一份声明中表示，此次和解是“历史上已知的最大的版权追回案”。 我们期待尽快向班级分发。”
